postCutoffAddenda.note: The original 2026-07-12 source-asset and fixed-view capture receipts remain unchanged. Planned hero/photo labels, next tests and current implementation status were corrected where later evidence disproved them. A separate 2026-07-16 database-free real-component capture is recorded as a new run; it proves pairwise visual differences, not a physical quality improvement or a product-route result.

runtimeProfileSecurityHardening.reviewedByteProfileReceiptLocation: API server only; absent from public response and production bundle
runtimeProfileSecurityHardening.authenticatedAdministratorPreview: separate private metadata and protected-stream path
runtimeProfileSecurityHardening.publicClientReceives: no Reception runtime profile or member URL; both reviewed byte profiles are structurally blocked from anonymous presentation

anonymousPresentationEligibility.reason: Neither reviewed byte profile has an exact reviewed immutable presentation contract.

futurePresentationContractRequirements: browser applies every reviewed value exactly rather than using local presentation defaults
runtimeProfileSecurityHardening.legacyBrowserResolver: retired to safe fallback because URL-mode Spark cannot attach an administrator bearer token
runtimeProfileSecurityHardening.legacyPublicSingleVisualPolicy: retired for every room; each room requires a reviewed immutable presentation contract in addition to reviewed bytes
runtimeProfileSecurityHardening.trustedOriginRule: PUBLIC_API_ORIGIN required and clean HTTPS in production; server ignores request Host for anonymous member URLs and client pins all four URLs to configured API origin
publicMetadataAndMemberRequestGates: exact reviewed byte identity and immutable presentation contract
every required QA check is present and passed
QA and transform match exact package/venue/room
same immutable package revision and member immediately before send
runtimeProfileSecurityHardening.runtimeProfileStorage: dedicated private runtime-profile R2 credentials and bucket; no public URL field, no fallback to legacy upload or Foundry storage, and a distinct bucket name is required
runtimeProfileSecurityHardening.immutableReviewIds: transform-artifact and QA-record IDs permit exact idempotent retries only; changed content requires a new ID
runtimeProfileSecurityHardening.physicalObjectVerification: cache misses read the complete private object and verify registered byte count and SHA-256; cache hits are keyed by the same SHA-256 and size; every request reruns all release gates and exact member identity immediately before send

verifiedByteCache.singleFlight: identical SHA-256 and size only; authorization remains per request

transferAdmission.absoluteDeadlineAction: abort upstream work and destroy the stalled response
transferAdmission.slotRelease: after both handler work and response finish/close or forced-deadline settlement

runtimeProfileSecurityHardening.privatePreviewDisconnectRule: The administrator-only private-preview byte route registers disconnect handling before database/package lookup, preventing a lookup-time disconnect from later acquiring or stranding a verified-transfer slot. Private byte preview does not grant anonymous presentation eligibility.
runtimeProfileSecurityHardening.memberRouteRateLimit: 24 requests per minute per client IP; edge/WAF controls remain required for distributed traffic
externalActivationRequirements: review and bind the exact group transform, camera policy/route and renderer-profile digest
make the browser apply the reviewed presentation values exactly
provision dedicated least-privilege private runtime-profile R2 credentials and bucket
copy and byte-verify exact reviewed objects
prove direct anonymous/public bucket access is denied
install edge rate-limit and WAF controls
keep public showcase disabled until every activation gate passes

latestStructuralComputerVision.plainLanguage: Computer vision found nine wall planes and two independent wall directions in each real input, but neither input proved one continuous Reception floor physically contacting the room walls. No room height, transform, overlay or approval was created.
latestStructuralComputerVision.sourceNaming: The 20.5 GB, 149-scan file is venue-wide. Reception is a candidate station subset, not the whole E57. The staged F: copy and the earlier registered F:\E57 path share recorded size/mtime, but this addendum does not assert newly reverified byte identity.
latestStructuralComputerVision.nextTest: Supply a human-reviewed room-only 3D crop or floor-boundary polygon, then rerun fit-only structural CV with validation held back and frozen test stations untouched. Independent controls remain required for authority.

latestMatchedCameraComputerVision.plainLanguage: Computer vision now aligns the XGRIDS/Potree room to E57 and compares Quality and Mobile from three evidence-backed E57 cameras. The frozen primary rule gives Quality a slight directional lead, but the effect has no calibrated practical threshold and the largest allowed border sensitivity produces no reliable winner. Keep Quality as the review leader; do not promote or physically approve either candidate from this evidence alone.

scope.holdoutQualification: Earlier repository diagnostics contain some of these station records, so this is a method-specific holdout claim rather than a claim that the data has never been inspected anywhere.

latestMatchedCameraComputerVision.nextTest: Freeze this exact camera and scoring protocol, then run it once on method-specific holdout stations 126, 129 and 141 without changing thresholds. Independent surveyed controls or a fresh separately captured photographic survey remain required for physical accuracy.

latestLocalRealComponentPreflight.plainLanguage: The real Living Hall scene loaded both reviewed four-file candidates at the same six cameras. Computer vision found review-worthy differences in five views and could not reliably assess the low-edge ceiling view. This proves the candidates look different; without a registered real photograph or independently trusted scan it cannot say which is more accurate.
latestLocalRealComponentPreflight.assetIntegrityScope: The page and sidecars prove that the four named files loaded and that decoded totals matched. This capture manifest does not hash the bytes served by the local asset servers, so it is not a new byte-exact asset audit.
latestLocalRealComponentPreflight.viewGeometry: six feature framings sharing one optical centre; no orbit or moving camera

capture.staticRepeatMeaning: No still-frame pixel drift was observed after load. This does not prove moving-camera or orbit stability.
capture.rejectedPreliminaryCapture: An earlier screenshot path was reported to have returned JPEG bytes under .png names. Those discarded bytes and their rejection log/hash were not retained, so the incident is not independently reproducible; it was excluded from the metric set. The final run used Chrome DevTools Page.captureScreenshot, and all 24 final files independently pass PNG-signature, 1200x900, eight-bit-RGB and hash checks.

currentEnvironmentRecheck.meaning: The immutable capture artifacts remain valid, but today's whole app is not proved bit-reproducible from this selected environment list.

integrityVerification.environmentManifestScope: selected capture/render/CV files; not a complete transitive-module inventory

computerVision.interpretation: Similarity and edge-energy measurements locate differences. They do not establish physical truth, real detail or directional superiority.

verification.productionBuild: capture-era build passed with a temporary process-only dummy live-format Clerk key. Current production validation additionally requires a clean HTTPS VITE_API_URL; both validation-only values must stay process-local and must not be written to an environment file
verification.productionLeakScan: No development route, localhost asset URL, local candidate ID, local-preflight chunk or Reception splat payload was found; dist/splats/reception is absent.

limitations: no registered held-out photograph or independently trusted scan at the same cameras
all six cameras share one optical centre
no moving-camera, orbit, FPS, load-time, GPU-memory or named-device evidence
local development route only; protected authenticated route not captured
no human acceptance decision and no product quality improvement proved
latestLocalRealComponentPreflight.nextTest: Add spatially distinct near/mid/far/orbit and moving-camera performance evidence, then compare both candidates with a registered held-out physical reference. Separately authorize migrations, registration, deployment and authenticated streaming before repeating the protocol through the protected route.

postCaptureRuntimeDeliveryHardening.memberRouteRateLimit: 24 requests per minute per client IP; not a substitute for edge/WAF controls
postCaptureRuntimeDeliveryHardening.trustedOriginRule: clean production PUBLIC_API_ORIGIN only; request Host ignored and client pins all four member URLs to configured API origin
postCaptureRuntimeDeliveryHardening.privatePreviewRule: authenticated exact-package preview accepts only a non-null server-reviewed Quality or Mobile byte-profile label and protected streams; this grants no anonymous presentation eligibility. handling.externalShareRule: Create an approved redacted alias annex; do not distribute this raw register or screenshot bundle externally.

classificationPolicy.verified: Direct local inspection, a reproducible measurement, or an authoritative primary source.
classificationPolicy.supported_inference: Strongly supported by verified evidence, with a named decisive test outstanding.
classificationPolicy.hypothesis: A candidate mechanism awaiting a discriminating experiment.
classificationPolicy.blocked: A named artifact, authority, operator action, or implementation is absent.

assetFieldResolution.note: Every asset has a contextRef; shared camera/pose/frame facts are normalized here rather than repeated on all 50 records.

metricMethod.sampleDomain: uint8 sRGB RGB values normalized to [0,1], without linear-light conversion
metricMethod.scope: full 1200x900 frame including identical background; no feature mask
metricMethod.ssim: skimage-compatible 7x7 uniform window, sample covariance, K1=0.01, K2=0.03, data_range=1, reflect boundary, mean over RGB channels
metricMethod.mae: mean absolute error over every RGB sample

pairwiseResults.interpretation: Verified high pixel agreement at six tested Spark cameras; direct single-generation encoder provenance is not established; not a claim of physical/source quality.
Verified high PLY-to-SOG-package pixel agreement at six tested cameras; source/export settings and filtering remain confounders.
Verified high PLY-to-SPZ-package pixel agreement at six tested Spark cameras.
Same-Mobile-source SOG and SPZ valid fine packages render similarly at the six tested cameras; this does not rank them at spatially distinct cameras or motion.
Invalid parent-plus-child mounting visibly doubles/smears edges.
Coarse-only root is visibly softer than the fine frontier.
Sources materially differ; separate reconstruction/settings/SH/counts confound cause.

antiMetricFinding.result: Misranked invalid doubled edges as sharper.
antiMetricFinding.conclusion: Never use a single edge-energy metric as the quality gate.

limitations: No exact LCC Studio hero cameras
No non-Venviewer independent-viewer capture and no actual-product-route capture
No held-out reference photographs
No true training held-outs
No moving/orbit stability trace
All six automated framings share one optical centre; crop/FOV changes cannot test SH view dependence
Fixture route is not the complete product route; RR-INDEPENDENT-VIEWER-CAPTURE-SET and RR-ACTUAL-ROUTE-CAPTURE-SET remain
PSNR, SSIM and MAE were computed over the full 1200x900 RGB frame, including the identical background; feature masks are required for acceptance testing
fixedViewExperiment.nextTest: This block records the historical 2026-07-12 fixture experiment. The database-free real-component fixed A/B is now complete in latestLocalRealComponentPreflight. Current next tests are RR-LCC-HERO-CAPTURE-SET, RR-INDEPENDENT-VIEWER-CAPTURE-SET, spatially distinct moving/performance/held-out-reference evidence, and the separately authorized protected route.

coverage: doorway and room depth with a fixed small wall feature
repeatValidation.method: Build/freeze A and B from their respective nine mapping images plus the same independent controls; localize each paired validation photo pose-only against its corresponding frozen model; do not update either model.
repeatValidation.gate: Every pair localizes and agrees in room frame within 20 mm translation and 0.2 degrees rotation.

pilotDesign.e57Policy: Optional only; RR-E57-CROP-CONFIRMATION, RR-E57-METRIC-CONTROL and rights gates apply only if E57 is used.
pilotDesign.minimumPoseGate: At least 24/30 defensible poses and at least 8/9 mapping images in each subset; every repeat pair is mandatory.

rawGoNoGo.goScope: metadata inventory, pose/event preservation, and official vendor export request only

remainingBlockers: trainer production-path E57 depth loss wiring
candidate generation and runtime packaging; the separate downstream D-014 verifier is implemented and tested

runpod.limitations: The image/template/live pod were not launched or verified. The legacy runner now exits 78 before reaching its dormant obsolete trainer/config block; the runbook contains stale price assumptions. Secret/account/template state was not exposed or inferred.
runpod.nextTest: Complete local non-training import/config proofs without secrets, then request explicit approval for one capped RunPod smoke through the accepted D-016 activation route.

postCutoffCapabilityReconciliation.mcmc: An exact compiled Config B contract now exists, but no runtime worker consumes it.
postCutoffCapabilityReconciliation.depthSupervision: Strict depth-indexing and input-validation fixtures now pass, but the production loss remains unwired.
postCutoffCapabilityReconciliation.evaluation: A strict downstream held-out candidate verifier is implemented and tested, but the trainer does not produce its required candidate inputs or optimizer metrics.
postCutoffCapabilityReconciliation.manifestAndPackaging: The downstream D-014 verifier is real; candidate generation, bilateral serialization, resume and trainer-to-runtime packaging remain incomplete.

pipelineAudit.nextTest: Finish local non-training import/config proofs, then—only with explicit approval—run one capped RunPod smoke through the D-016 path. Local Windows training is not the execution route.

runtimeAudit.dominantFaultContext: verified in the historical/dev-fixture route; later code rejects mixed replacement levels, and a fresh database-free real-component fixed-view run exists, but no protected-route or physical-reference capture proves product quality
runtimeAudit.sourceSubstitution: current pointer changed from earlier Quality SH3 SOG to later Mobile SH0 SPZ
runtimeAudit.sourceQualityEffect: unproved_direction; separate reconstructions and missing physical/cross-context hero references confound the comparison
runtimeAudit.conditionalPublicContractRisk: if the current multi-file package is published through the one-URL schema, it resolves only the coarse root and cannot express the reviewed fine frontier
secondaryFaults: legacy/general renderer paths remain partly unpinned; the Reception fixed-review profile is explicit, but blur optimization and wider product adoption are unproved
planner tests still pin DPR 0.75; resolver tests now reject mixed replacement levels
runtimeAudit.sparkSpzCompatibility: up to SPZ v3 in inspected Spark2; test any SPZ v4 output
runtimeAudit.nextTest: The database-free real-component still-image preflight is complete. Next add spatially distinct moving views, FPS/load/GPU/device evidence and a registered held-out physical reference. Separately, after authorized migrations, registration, deployment and streaming, repeat through the protected authenticated product route.

postCutoffStatus.localAcceptanceBlocker: spatially distinct moving-camera, FPS/load/GPU/device and held-out physical-reference evidence; the controlled blur/pre-blur baseline is not proved optimal
postCutoffStatus.protectedRouteAcceptanceBlocker: migration/deployment approval, internal-ready candidate registration, authenticated streaming, reviewed transform, and fresh protected-route fixed/moving capture

attachedMeshAudit.limitations: The attached mesh adds a small geometry-only mesh, not a higher-detail splat or photographic texture. It was not rendered in the six-view pure-splat matrix.
attachedMeshAudit.nextTest: Render mesh-only and hybrid at column/skirting/cornice detail once the transform is reviewed; reject if silhouettes, lighting or occlusion mismatch.

e57ReceptionCandidate.boundaryObservation: scan 121 appears to be the doorway
e57ReceptionCandidate.nextTest: Create a human-reviewed room-only 3D crop or Reception floor-boundary polygon; preserve scan/top/side evidence and obtain a second-person check. A scan-ID screenshot alone is insufficient.

existingColmapAudit.scope: Existing model/materialized faces cover E57 scans 0-49 only.
existingColmapAudit.limitations: It cannot be treated as a Reception registration scaffold and its venue/room identity does not transfer by proximity or filename.
existingColmapAudit.nextTest: After RR-E57-CROP-CONFIRMATION, RR-E57-METRIC-CONTROL and the applicable Matterport rights decision, optionally create a new Reception-only scaffold from scans 122-144 and preserve fixed source poses.

commercialSources.claim: LCC Studio v2.1.0 is current and dated 2026-07-02; release history describes relevant alignment/HD changes.
Current Single Model HD requirements include same-device JPG/PNG/JPEG, >8 GB VRAM, and PortalCam photo/control-point limits.
Current FAQ recommends approximately 80 percent overlap, fixed focal/resolution, no zoom/crop, sharp/fixed-exposure images over 1024x768 and roughly 100-degree horizontal FOV.
commercialSources.limitations: FAQ and Single Model pages conflict on the generic 500/1000 image ceiling; ask vendor.

commercialSources.claim: Official Shopify feed displayed Premium variant 43558032998493 at USD 2500/year with available=true.
commercialSources.limitations: Human page/feed checkout availability conflicted; entitlement, tax, devices and output rights unverified.

commercialSources.claim: Official Shopify feed displayed current-period PortalCam upgrade LCC variant 44177759502429 at USD 1000 with available=true.
commercialSources.limitations: Actual eligibility and remaining term need written confirmation.

commercialSources.claim: Official Shopify feed displayed LCC Cloud variant 44461078904925 at USD 800/year with available=true; public material reviewed did not establish HD Enhancement scope.
commercialSources.limitations: Reference amount only; checkout, tax, availability and HD entitlement require confirmation.

commercialSources.claim: Public XGRIDS Terms do not clearly settle locally generated output ownership/distribution in the reviewed text.
commercialSources.limitations: Not legal advice; no visible stable version/effective date or retained immutable content snapshot was recorded; supplemental/order terms may control. LEGAL-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT-SET remains.

commercialSources.claim: RunPod displayed RTX 4090 USD 0.69/h, L40S 0.99/h, A100 PCIe 1.39/h and A100 SXM 1.49/h.
commercialSources.limitations: Availability, tax, storage, price changes and secure-region choice apply.

commercialSources.claim: RealityScan is USD 1250/user/year unless an EULA exception applies; the revenue exception uses previous-12-month corporate-group gross revenue and includes advances or funds raised, with separate educational/non-commercial rules.
commercialSources.limitations: Eligibility and input/source rights still require review. The mutable EULA showed no retained version date/content snapshot; LEGAL-SOURCE-SNAPSHOT-SET remains.

commercialSources.claim: ArtiFixer released checkpoint is research/development only under NVIDIA One-Way Noncommercial terms.

conclusions.claim: Invalid parent-plus-child LCC2 mounting is the strongest measured legacy/dev-fixture runtime-contract loss; the older conditional one-URL path would instead select only the coarse root. The later private exact route rejects mixed levels but has not been deployed or captured.
conclusions.limitation: Measured in the Spark fixture; actual-product-route and physical hero references remain missing.

conclusions.claim: The runtime substituted a separate Mobile SH0 reconstruction for the higher-capacity Quality SH3 candidate, and the two sources render materially differently; directional source-quality superiority is unproved.
conclusions.limitation: Separate reconstructions, settings, cameras, SH capacity and physical-reference absence confound the comparison.

conclusions.claim: PLY-to-valid-SOG/SPZ-package pixel deltas are much smaller than the invalid-LOD and coarse-only deltas at six tested same-centre Spark framings.
conclusions.limitation: Original encoder version/settings, single-generation lineage, filtering, spatially distinct cameras, alpha/SH behaviour, motion, and physical reference remain.
conclusions.nextTest: Recover exporter provenance and run actual-route spatially distinct/moving views plus photographic references.

conclusions.claim: No authorized/open independent-retraining path is established from the reviewed Reception PortalCam artifacts.
conclusions.limitation: An official uninspected export/SDK may change the verdict.

conclusions.claim: An owned-photography/trainer lane is technically viable enough for a bounded 30-photo registration gate; E57 inclusion is optional and rights/control-gated.
conclusions.limitation: Control network, E57 room identity/accuracy/rights, registration performance and trainer execution are unproved.
conclusions.nextTest: Capture controls, rights memo, 30-photo gate and trainer smoke; add E57 only if separately approved.

conclusions.claim: Subject to existing-base distribution rights, a corrected higher-capacity vendor candidate plus separately captured hero layers is the strongest practical primary plan to test.
conclusions.limitation: No hero micro-splat or vendor HD candidate has yet been produced; vendor-base shipping rights are not established.
conclusions.nextTest: Same-photo vendor HD versus independent hero/full candidate bake-off.

conclusions.claim: Three-dimensional computer vision can extract strong wall and horizontal-surface evidence from both real captures, but the current evidence cannot establish one common Reception room envelope or an approved E57-to-XGRIDS transform.
conclusions.limitation: The fit-only rerun intentionally read no validation, frozen test or excluded station geometry and wrote no output. It did not rehash the entire venue-wide E57.
conclusions.nextTest: RR-E57-CROP-CONFIRMATION followed by held-back validation and independent physical controls.

conclusions.claim: The real Living Hall component can load the reviewed Quality SOG and Mobile SPZ four-file fine candidates at exact observed decoded totals, and the two candidates produce reproducible pairwise visual differences at six same-centre feature views.
conclusions.limitation: The capture does not re-hash the served asset bytes, use a registered held-out physical reference, test spatially distinct or moving cameras, measure performance/GPU/device behaviour, or run through the protected authenticated route. It proves difference, not directional quality or product improvement.
conclusions.nextTest: Add registered physical-reference, near/mid/far/orbit, moving-camera and performance evidence, then separately repeat through the authorized protected route.
